content.full_text:
April 23, 1976


Mr. Bill Akers
Senate Select Committee on
Small Business Enterprises
State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Bill:

Thank you for your letter of April 23 requesting information concerning Senator David Roberti's obligations under Section 89001 of the Political Reform Act.

The Commission presently is considering a regulation which will further define the scope of Section 89001. Pending the adoption of that regulation, the Commission has indicated that the proposed action by Senator Roberti, while acting in his official capacity as chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Small Business Enterprises, would not be in violation of the law. Moreover, Senator Roberti would not be prohibited from signing the letter if it is clear that he is signing on behalf of the committee.

I must caution you, however, that this represents a preliminary position of the Commission and that its final action on the pending regulation may be different. If the Commission does change its policy, the change will have prospective effect only and will not affect this preliminary advise.

If you wish additional information, please do not hesitate to call on me.

Very truly yours,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:mak

bcc: Ken Finney with attachments.

[The document appears to be typed text that is significantly faded and difficult to read clearly. Much of the content is obscured or reversed/mirrored in the image. Without being able to reliably make out the specific words with confidence, providing a verbatim transcription would result in numerous [illegible] markers or potentially inaccurate text. The document appears to be several paragraphs long and may be a formal letter or official document, but the image quality and fading make accurate character-by-character transcription unreliable.]

COMMITTEE ADDRESS
1115 9TH STREET, 271
9TH AND L BUILDING
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
916/322-3960

CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE

SENATE SELECT COMMITTEE
ON
SMALL BUSINESS ENTERPRISES

DAVID A. ROBERTI
CHAIRMAN

April 23, 1976

Regina Kutchins
Fair Political Practices Commission
1110 K Street
Sacramento, California

Dear Regina:

The Senate Select Committee on Small Business Enterprises
occasionally submits information and questions to an advisory
committee composed of approximately 250 members. The advisory
committee is made up of businessman, educators, and local off-
icials from throughout California.

From glancing at a roster of advisory committee members, it
seems that a very small percentage reside in Senator Roberti's
27th Senatorial District (see attached list).

We are preparing a summary of legislation which affects the
small businessman, and will submit this information to the
advisory committee in one mailing. Throughout the course of
the legislative session, we will also ask that the advisory
committee their opinion on pending legislation, and request
their input on problems and possible solutions which relate
to small business.

QUESTION: Will the Senate Committee on Small Business Enterprises
(SBE) be in violation of FPPC rules and guidelines if
it sends a mailing to its advisory committee? The
mailing will strictly relate to committee business,
and will be sent on committee letterhead (Senator
Roberti's name is on the top).

Note: Senator Roberti has declared his candidacy
and has filed his papers with the county
clerk (Los Angeles County).

If the mailing is determined to comply with FPPC
guidelines, can Senator Roberti sign the cover
letter of the mailing?

Sincerely,

Bill Akers

sections.question:
Will the Senate Committee on Small Business Enterprises (SBE) be in violation of FPPC rules and guidelines if it sends a mailing to its advisory committee? The mailing will strictly relate to committee business, and will be sent on committee letterhead (Senator Roberti's name is on the top).

Note: Senator Roberti has declared his candidacy and has filed his papers with the county clerk (Los Angeles County).

If the mailing is determined to comply with FPPC guidelines, can Senator Roberti sign the cover letter of the mailing?

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Will the Senate Committee on Small Business Enterprises (SBE) be in violation of FPPC rules and guidelines if it sends a mailing to its advisory committee? The mailing will strictly relate to committee business, and will be sent on committee letterhead (Senator Roberti's name is on the top).

Note: Senator Roberti has declared his candidacy and has filed his papers with the county clerk (Los Angeles County).

If the mailing is determined to comply with FPPC guidelines, can Senator Roberti sign the cover letter of the mailing?
embedding.first_500_words: April 23, 1976 Mr. Bill Akers Senate Select Committee on Small Business Enterprises State Capitol Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Bill: Thank you for your letter of April 23 requesting information concerning Senator David Roberti's obligations under Section 89001 of the Political Reform Act. The Commission presently is considering a regulation which will further define the scope of Section 89001. Pending the adoption of that regulation, the Commission has indicated that the proposed action by Senator Roberti, while acting in his official capacity as chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Small Business Enterprises, would not be in violation of the law. Moreover, Senator Roberti would not be prohibited from signing the letter if it is clear that he is signing on behalf of the committee. I must caution you, however, that this represents a preliminary position of the Commission and that its final action on the pending regulation may be different. If the Commission does change its policy, the change will have prospective effect only and will not affect this preliminary advise. If you wish additional information, please do not hesitate to call on me. Very truly yours, REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS Technical Assistance Program Coordinator RSK:mak bcc: Ken Finney with attachments. Dear Bill: I hope you will find this information, please do not hesitate to call on me. If you have significant information, please do not hesitate to send it to the Secretary, the agenda will have prospective effect only unless the policy, the operation may be different. ROBERTI CHAIRMAN April 23, 1976 Regina Kutchins Fair Political Practices Commission 1110 K Street Sacramento, California Dear Regina: The Senate Select Committee on Small Business Enterprises occasionally submits information and questions to an advisory committee composed of approximately 250 members. The advisory committee is made up of businessmen, educators, and local officials from throughout California. From glancing at a roster of advisory committee members, it seems that a very small percentage reside in Senator Roberti's 27th Senatorial District (see attached list). We are preparing a summary of legislation which affects the small businessman, and
